xposts.title: Consciousness as the dream of dreaming
xposts.excerpt: Consciousness is the dream of dreaming. It establishes the perception that percepts are being perceived. Consciousness is a type of representation, a pattern that gains efficacy by an interpreter that translates it into actions that extend and modify the pattern.

xposts.title: Experience as virtual reality simulation
xposts.excerpt: Your experience is a simulation, a virtual reality projected into a virtual observer. The observer is a model of what it would be like if you existed, were uploaded into a monkey and deeply invested in its affairs. The model is used to drive a monkey in the physical universe.

xposts.title: Conscious observer as simulated agent
xposts.excerpt: It seems pretty clear that we, the conscious observer, are a simulation, of what it would be like if we existed as a unified agent, perceiving the models our brain generates and cared about them. Consciousness is simulated.

xposts.title: Phenomenal experience as nervous system models
xposts.excerpt: Phenomenal experience is not of things that exist but of model states represented in the nervous system. There exists an implementation that produces the simulation of mental experience, by manipulating representations.

xposts.title: Dream memories instantiated wholesale
xposts.excerpt: I have had long dream memories after seconds of sleep, which suggests that memories can be instantiated wholesale, instead of living through them sequentially. Not every mental computation corresponds to a conscious experience
